introSection:
El Loco is a steel wild mouse indoor roller coaster located at Adventuredome in Las Vegas, Nevada, USA. It was built by S&S Worldwide. Opened in 2014, it is the sixth El Loco installation and the second to be built in the United States. The ride features two inversions and single car trains.

The attraction is located where the splashdown for Rim Runner, an Arrow Dynamics boat ride, previously stood.

Unlike all previous El Loco installations, the ride does not have a "beyond-vertical" drop. Instead, the drop is approximately 90 degrees steep.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The addition of El Loco was announced in early 2013.

Track arrived on-site in early September and the final piece was installed on December 12, 2013. It was opened on February 18, 2014.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: El Loco has yellow track and black supports. The layout differs to that of other El Loco installations from by S&S.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The ride has trains with lap-bars and on-board sound.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: A woman fell from the ride on March 25, 2019. The woman was a double amputee. The ride reopened by August 6, 2019 with updated rider restrictions.

fullText:
El Loco is a steel wild mouse indoor roller coaster located at Adventuredome in Las Vegas, Nevada, USA. It was built by S&S Worldwide. Opened in 2014, it is the sixth El Loco installation and the second to be built in the United States. The ride features two inversions and single car trains.

The attraction is located where the splashdown for Rim Runner, an Arrow Dynamics boat ride, previously stood.

Unlike all previous El Loco installations, the ride does not have a "beyond-vertical" drop. Instead, the drop is approximately 90 degrees steep.

The addition of El Loco was announced in early 2013.

Track arrived on-site in early September and the final piece was installed on December 12, 2013. It was opened on February 18, 2014.

El Loco has yellow track and black supports. The layout differs to that of other El Loco installations from by S&S.

The ride has trains with lap-bars and on-board sound.

A woman fell from the ride on March 25, 2019. The woman was a double amputee. The ride reopened by August 6, 2019 with updated rider restrictions.
